items.title: Enhance BrowserDevice Management UI in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive owner-facing UI for managing BrowserDevice pairing and secure exact-claim processes. Key features include challenge code handling, real-time expiry management, and the ability to rename or revoke devices. The UI is designed to enhance user experience while maintaining strict security protocols, ensuring that ordinary users cannot access the pairing flow without the appropriate feature flag.

items.title: Transition to Local Browser Execution for Xiaohongshu in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the unlaunched Xiaohongshu cloud runtime with a local browser execution skill. Key changes include the removal of over 7,800 lines of cloud code, implementing strict local account verification, and ensuring that media publishing remains blocked until local upload capabilities are established. The migration is designed to enhance performance and security while maintaining user control.

items.title: Launch Browser Relay Skeleton in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the foundational structure for the authenticated Browser Relay service, enabling outbound-only WebSocket connections for registered browser devices. Key features include P-256/JCS authentication, Redis integration, and a robust security model with strict origin policies. The deployment is validated with successful unit tests and integration checks, paving the way for future enhancements.

items.title: Resolve Migration Conflicts in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a no-op Django merge migration to resolve conflicts between the `0214_restore_veo_extend_api` and `0215_order_finished_at` migrations. It also unblocks the PlatformBackend init container, which was previously failing due to multiple migration leaf nodes. The changes ensure smoother deployment and stability in the backend environment.

items.title: Exciting New Omni Video Module Added in Nexior 🎥
items.summary: We've introduced a brand new standalone Omni video module to Nexior, enabling text-to-video, image-to-video, and video editing capabilities powered by omni-flash. This module follows the established standalone video pattern and integrates seamlessly with the existing gemini services. Key components include a new configuration panel, recent tasks display, and support for multiple languages.

items.title: Enhance BrowserDevice Pairing and Relay Bootstrap in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive BrowserDevice pairing state machine, including P-256 proof and owner confirmation. Key features such as signed resumable completion polling, PostgreSQL-backed public rate limits, and two-phase Relay bootstrap introspection have been added. The changes ensure secure credential handling and allow for schema deployment without immediate feature activation.

items.title: Integrate BrowserAgent as a Submodule in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update registers the `AceDataCloud/BrowserAgent` repository as a top-level submodule in the Index, tracking the main branch. The integration includes a pinned commit for consistency and validation checks have confirmed the integrity of the setup. The `.gitmodules` file has been updated accordingly to reflect this new addition.

items.title: Enhance Task Media Display in Nexior 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces the display of input images, videos, and audio for tasks in Kling, enhancing user experience. It includes support for various media types and improves task rendering by safeguarding against malformed video list data. The changes have been validated with comprehensive testing and linting.

items.title: Revamped TTAPI Pricing and Features in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update restores the `/veo/extend` endpoint to public access and lowers the pricing for Veo generation. The new rates are set at 1.68 credits for fast and 12.00 credits for quality, aligning with TTAPI's native extension support. Additionally, optional fields for aspect ratio and resolution have been added to the OpenAPI documentation.

items.title: Enhance TTAPI with Veo Video Extension Support in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces a new `POST /veo/extend` endpoint to the TTAPI worker, allowing users to extend Veo video tasks while preserving existing contracts. Key improvements include user verification for source tasks, model matching, and aspect ratio inheritance. The rollout plan ensures a smooth transition with minimal disruption to existing services.

items.title: Launch of Read-Only BrowserAgent MVP in Browser Agent 🚀
items.summary: The new read-only MVP for the BrowserAgent has been successfully implemented, featuring a fixed extension ID, managed tab isolation, and strict security measures. Key functionalities include a non-extractable P-256 identity and fail-closed tool calls until authentication capabilities are established. The code has passed extensive validation, including audits and tests, ensuring a robust foundation for future enhancements.

items.title: Revamp Marketing Dashboard for Admins in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update transforms the existing Marketing Attribution page into a comprehensive Marketing Dashboard. Key features include KPI cards for signups and revenue, a conversion funnel, and synchronized date filters. The dashboard is now fully localized across all supported languages, enhancing accessibility for users worldwide.

items.title: Launch Marketing Analytics API in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new admin-only marketing analytics API that tracks signup cohorts, user funnels, and revenue metrics. Key features include enhanced attribution for shared credentials and the ability to analyze monthly active users over a trailing 30-day period. Additionally, new database migrations ensure accurate revenue tracking and historical data integrity during deployments.

items.title: Refresh Featured Models for Enhanced Visibility in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update promotes GPT-5.6 Luna, Terra, and Sol, along with Gemini 3.5 Flash, in the public model directory. Additionally, GPT Image 2 and Kling v3 Omni are now featured, while GPT-5.5 has been retired from the Featured list. The changes ensure that the catalog reflects the latest and most relevant models for users.

items.title: New Memory Preference Toggle for Chat Settings in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a memory settings switch that allows users to enable or disable long-term memory in chats. The feature is backed by an account-level preference and ensures that existing memories are preserved even when disabled. Additionally, conflicts with ScheduledTasks have been resolved, and various linting issues have been addressed.

items.title: Enhance Pricing Conditions Display in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant improvements to the pricing conditions display for Seedance and Midjourney. It adds explicit columns for reference video options and consolidates surcharge conditions to eliminate redundancy. Additionally, the new utility for JsonLogic condition extraction enhances maintainability and testing across all supported locales.

items.title: Enhance conversation revisions with CAS support in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces monotonic conversation revisions and compare-and-swap (CAS) writes across various operations, including sync, async, and CRUD paths. A rollout flag is set to false by default to maintain legacy functionality until a coordinated maintenance window is executed. Additionally, it implements typed 409 conflict responses and includes extensive tests to ensure stability and performance.

items.title: Enhance Aichat2 Memory Management in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces persistent memory preferences for users, allowing them to opt-out of memory features across chat prompts and tools. Key changes include enforcing account preferences, preserving settings during memory clearance, and implementing atomic write operations to prevent race conditions. Additionally, the latest Prettier fixes have been applied to ensure a clean linting process.

items.title: Enhance Browser Execution States in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces read-only chat rendering for durable BrowserDevice execution states, enhancing user experience without requiring cloud approval controls. Key features include strict state normalization, early-event buffering, and a dedicated activity card. The implementation has passed a comprehensive test suite, ensuring stability across various builds and locales.

items.title: Add Reference Video Uploader for Omni Editing in Nexior 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces a Reference Video uploader in the Kling config panel, enabling users to utilize the `kling-video-o1` Omni video editing feature effectively. The new component allows for the upload of MP4/MOV files, enhancing the editing capabilities by automatically managing prompt tokens and ensuring a seamless user experience. Additionally, internationalization support has been added for 18 locales.

items.title: Add omni-flash video pricing to Gemini service page in Backend 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces the omni-flash pricing rule to the Gemini service display-pricing file, ensuring that the video generation and editing price is visible on the pricing page. Previously, users could not see the pricing for the omni-flash model, which is now aligned with the existing billing rules. This change enhances user transparency regarding video service costs.

items.title: Enhance phone confirmation handling in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update teaches the shared login classifier to recognize phone confirmation as a 'logging_in' state, improving the accuracy of account-selection matching. It also prevents the standalone watchdog and quick-login callers from clicking during phone confirmation, ensuring a smoother user experience. Comprehensive tests confirm that the changes are effective and do not introduce regressions.

items.title: Introducing BrowserDevice and Session Management in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a foundational `BrowserDevice` identity and introduces browser session connection/auth modes, which are initially disabled by default. Key features include owner-scoped device management with idempotent soft revoke, enhanced token handling for browser sessions, and a new Browser Relay readiness contract. The changes ensure a robust and secure integration for browser-based connections.

items.title: Enhance Browser Session Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces improved handling for active browser sessions within the aichat2 framework, ensuring they are excluded from various processes such as token lookups and skill loading. Key files have been modified to implement these changes, and new tests have been added to validate the functionality. The changes maintain existing behaviors for OAuth and other connection types, paving the way for future enhancements.

items.title: Introduce Memory Toggle for Aichat2 Requests in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a new `memory_enabled` flag to Aichat2 requests, allowing users to control memory features. When set to false, memory prompt injection, memory tools, and background learning are disabled, enhancing user control over memory usage. The change is backward-compatible and has been validated with successful tests.

items.title: Enhance Error Localization in Nexior 🗣️
items.summary: This update localizes the error messages for scheduled tasks, ensuring that all 13 distinct failure codes are now properly translated across 18 supported locales. Key changes include the addition of localized messages for the `billing_gate_failed` error and improvements to the rendering of error messages in the task cards. This enhances user experience by providing clear and understandable error notifications in their native languages.

items.title: Upgrade AuthFrontend for Better Control Sizing in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update upgrades AuthFrontend from `@acedatacloud/core` version 0.16 to 0.18, allowing Element Plus to manage control sizing effectively. The change aligns button sizes with Studio's native 32px defaults, eliminating the need for local workarounds. Validation checks confirm the successful implementation of this upgrade.

items.title: Enhance Mountsea Concurrency Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of upstream concurrency limits in Mountsea by detecting saturation through HTTP 429 and nested 'too_many_requests' responses. It maps these errors to the existing BusyError(500) contract, ensuring smoother failover routing and providing clearer public messages instead of generic object representations. Additionally, it includes tests to validate the new error mapping functionality.

items.title: Enhance Console Scroll Behavior in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the console's scroll behavior by ensuring that the root scroll area no longer expands unnecessarily. Key changes include the addition of paint containment for the desktop layout, which prevents the document root from scrolling independently of the console. This fix enhances the user experience by maintaining a clean and consistent interface across different screen sizes.

items.title: Streamline Application Responses for Efficiency in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a compact service projection for the application list endpoint, significantly reducing response sizes. The new projection decreases uncompressed data from over 3.8MB to approximately 362KB, and gzip sizes from around 657KB to about 44KB. Regression tests have been added to ensure both the compact and full detail contracts remain intact.

items.title: Restored Admin Invoice Icon in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update adds the `faFileInvoice` icon to the centralized icon library and restores its display next to the admin Invoices navigation item. The changes ensure that the icon is properly registered and available for use, enhancing the user interface for admin functionalities.

items.title: Fix video storage path for omni-flash outputs in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update changes the storage path for omni-flash video outputs from the shared '/sora' folder to the dedicated '/gemini' folder. The fix involves adding a new constant for the gemini path and updating the relevant transfer function call. Existing '/sora' URLs remain functional, ensuring no disruption for past records.

items.title: Enhance API Debugging Skills in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `debug-api-case` skill designed to assist in diagnosing API requests by tracing various identifiers and logs. It establishes a comprehensive workflow for clue-to-trace and forensic reporting, enabling detailed reconstruction of user interactions and root cause analysis. Additionally, a reusable report template has been added to standardize findings and recommendations.

items.title: Enhance Model Support Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update dynamically builds the supported-model catalog from MongoDB capabilities and returns a 400 error for unsupported models. It preserves the 500 error for temporarily invalid accounts and removes the historical account truncation while ensuring safe catalog reads. Key improvements include better handling of model fallbacks and case insensitivity.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Watchdog Supervision in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new standalone watchdog supervisor for the WeChat integration, ensuring robust monitoring and automatic restarts every 15 seconds. Key changes include the addition of a PowerShell script for health checks and modifications to the startup process to prevent multiple watchdog instances. The implementation has passed extensive testing, confirming its reliability and effectiveness.

items.title: Revive aichat2 Tool Calls for GPT-5.5 in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update restores functionality for the aichat2 tool calls when using GPT-5.5 by modifying the responses shim. Key changes include converting tool calls to the Responses API format and ensuring proper handling of function call arguments. The implementation has been thoroughly tested, passing all tests and aligning with OpenAI's Responses API documentation.

items.title: Enhance Gemini CLI with Video Options in Clis 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces new features to the Gemini CLI, including a resolution option for existing commands and a new video-to-video command. Users can now specify video resolutions (720p or 1080p) and utilize the new command to transform videos with specified URLs. These enhancements improve the flexibility and usability of the CLI for video processing tasks.

items.title: Fix: preserve translated json quotes in Backend 🐛
Enhance Video Editing Documentation in Backend 🎥
items.summary: This update documents the video editing capabilities of the `/gemini/videos` API, introducing optional parameters for `video_urls` and `resolution`. The changes include refined descriptions in the OpenAPI spec and additional documentation in Chinese, ensuring clarity for users. This enhancement streamlines video editing processes without the need for a separate catalog.

items.title: Enhance Video Editing Capabilities in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces video editing features directly on the existing `/gemini/videos` route, allowing users to upload and edit videos with optional parameters for `video_urls` and `resolution`. The changes streamline the process by eliminating the need for a separate `/omni/videos` service, enhancing efficiency and user experience. Validation improvements ensure that only valid inputs are processed, further solidifying the functionality.

items.title: Align Edit Support Contacts Dialog with Element Plus Sizes in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: The Edit Support Contacts dialog has been updated to remove explicit small size attributes from various controls, ensuring consistency with the default Element Plus sizing used throughout the UI. This change enhances the visual coherence of the dialog with the rest of the application, as it now matches the native control sizes. No other logic or styling changes were made.

items.title: Expanded Semantic Icon Set in Common Frontend 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive semantic icon manifest with 189 reviewed product meanings. The new static, tree-shakeable entry point `@acedatacloud/core/icons/components` exposes all semantics while preserving existing single-icon subpaths. The release is tagged as immutable version `@acedatacloud/core@0.18.0`, ensuring compatibility and stability.

items.title: Enhance SMS Webhook Configuration in Nexior 🔧
items.summary: This update introduces a customizable SMS webhook configuration in the Auth panel, allowing site owners to set their own SMS sender endpoint. Key features include a toggle for enabling custom endpoints, updated documentation access, and a testing interface for connectivity checks. The changes enhance user control over SMS delivery while ensuring proper localization across multiple languages.

items.title: Enhance SMS Webhook Testing with Owner Validation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new endpoint for testing SMS webhooks, allowing site owners to verify their configurations before going live. Key features include authentication checks to ensure only site owners can access the endpoint, rate limiting per user, and input validation to prevent abuse. The changes enhance security and usability for the SMS delivery system in Nexior.

items.title: Enhance Auto-Recharge UI with VIP Discounts in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update addresses user feedback by ensuring the auto-recharge UI displays the correct VIP credit discount. The front end now relies on backend data to show the discounted price, eliminating any confusion regarding pricing. Key changes include updates to the auto-recharge modal and list page, along with internationalization adjustments for multiple languages.

items.title: Streamline Auto-Recharge Pricing Logic in Backend ⚡
items.summary: This update introduces a unified pricing mechanism for auto-recharge by implementing the `quote_recharge` function, which standardizes the calculation of charges and discounts. Key changes include modifications to serializers and views to ensure that the displayed price matches the actual charge, enhancing consistency and reliability. Additionally, new tests have been added to validate the functionality and ensure robustness.
